macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stock Markets Plunge on March 13 Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Oil Surge
items.summary: The Dow Jones fell 1.6% to 46,677.85, its lowest close of 2026; S&P 500 dropped 1.5% to 6,672.62; Nasdaq declined 1.8% to 22,311.98, marking fifth straight day of losses for S&P amid Iran conflict and oil prices above $98/barrel.[1][2][3]
items.source: Zacks Investment Research, WA Trust, TheStreet

items.title: IEA Announces Historic 400 Million Barrel Oil Release as Saudi Arabia Ramps Up Production
items.summary: The International Energy Agency's 32 member countries agreed to release 400 million barrels of crude oil, the largest such action in its history, while OPEC noted Saudi Arabia increasing production to counter Middle East supply disruptions from Iran conflict and Strait of Hormuz issues.[1][2]

items.title: Market Expectations for Fed Rate Cuts Moderate to One in 2026 Amid Inflation Fears
items.summary: February CPI data overshadowed by March oil surge above $98/barrel, leading to stagflation concerns; markets now price in only one Federal Reserve rate cut later in 2026, down from prior expectations.[1]

items.title: Energy Sector Bucks Market Decline with 2.5% Gain
items.summary: The Energy Select Sector SPDR (XLE) rose 2.5% on March 13 despite broad market sell-off, driven by whiplash in oil prices amid geopolitical tensions.[2][3]

items.title: VIX Fear Gauge Surges 12.6% to 27.29 as Volatility Rises
items.summary: CBOE Volatility Index jumped amid choppy trading, headline-driven swings from Iran conflict, and oil volatility; decliners heavily outnumbered advancers on NYSE and Nasdaq.[2]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Kraken Becomes First Crypto Firm to Secure Federal Reserve Payment Access
items.summary: Kraken has gained access to Federal Reserve payment rails, marking a significant step for crypto integration into traditional finance. This development highlights accelerating Bitcoin adoption amid weak investor sentiment.

items.title: White House Backs Digital Asset Market Clarity Act, Boosting XRP Prospects
items.summary: The President is pushing the Clarity Act to position the US as the crypto capital, with Senate momentum building and Ripple praising the regulatory progress. This could treat XRP as a commodity, enabling institutional adoption and supply tightening via escrow locks.

items.title: Mastercard Launches Crypto Partner Program; Wells Fargo Explores Stablecoin
items.summary: Major institutions like Mastercard and Wells Fargo are deepening crypto involvement with new programs and stablecoin explorations. Regulators are coordinating oversight as lawmakers advance the Clarity Act.

items.title: Bitcoin Price Squeeze Ends Amid Technical Analysis Signals
items.summary: Bitcoin's price squeeze has concluded, with analysts noting potential breakouts or downside retests; Ethereum faces key support at $2,800-$2,100. Market discussions include rate cut expectations and BTC targets around $50K.

items.title: XRP and Clarity Act Gain Momentum with Institutional Interest in ETFs
items.summary: Growing interest in XRP ETFs and Mastercard's on-chain payment rails signal institutional execution in 2026. The Clarity Act is seen as pivotal for XRP Ledger integration with banks and tokenized assets.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Launches Anthropic Institute to Study AI's Societal Impacts
items.summary: Anthropic has launched the Anthropic Institute to examine AI's effects on jobs, governance, and security. The company warns that rapid AI advances could significantly reshape society.

items.title: AI Lab Revamps AI Coding Tool with New Executives from Cursor
items.summary: An AI lab is enhancing its AI coding tool efforts by bringing on two new executives from Cursor. This move aims to strengthen development in coding AI capabilities.

items.title: Technical Survey: GRPO, DAPO, RLVR Replace RLHF in 2026 Post-Training
items.summary: A new technical survey details how GRPO, DAPO, RLVR, and synthetic self-play have become the dominant post-training methods, replacing RLHF. It explores the changes and reasons behind this shift.

items.title: Gemini 3.1 Pro: Comprehensive Guide to Google's Advanced Pro-Tier Model
items.summary: Google DeepMind's Gemini 3.1 Pro features a 1M-token context window, 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2, and multimodal reasoning across text, images, audio, video, and code. It is accessible via Gemini API, Vertex AI, and Google Antigravity.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Xbox Announces Next-Gen Project Helix and Xbox Mode for Windows 11
items.summary: At GDC, Xbox's Jason Ronald revealed alpha versions of next-gen Project Helix for developers in 2027, supporting both console and PC games. Xbox Mode is coming to Windows 11 in select markets starting April, adding Xbox features to PCs.

items.title: Nvidia Enables 90 FPS VR Streaming on GeForce NOW from March 19
items.summary: GeForce NOW users can play games at up to 90 FPS on VR headsets like Apple Vision Pro and Meta Quest starting March 19, as announced at GDC.

items.title: CMF and Creative BC Invest $1M in Five B.C. Video Game Studios
items.summary: The Canada Media Fund and Creative BC announced a $1M joint investment in five British Columbia-based studios through the Video Games Business Development Program to support original games and business growth.

items.title: Time Crisis Turns 30 with Global G'AIM'E World Championship 2026
items.summary: Bandai Namco's Time Crisis celebrates its 30th anniversary with an online tournament starting March 14, 2026, where players compete for fastest clear times and prizes like $500 Amazon gift cards.

items.title: Fortnite's Original Save the World Mode Goes Free-to-Play After Nine Years
items.summary: Epic Games is making Fortnite's original PVE survival mode, Save the World, free-to-play after nearly nine years.

items.title: Fallout 3 Remaster Rumors Fueled by McFarlane Toys Nuka-Cola Listing
items.summary: Speculation about a Fallout 3 remaster intensifies after McFarlane Toys listed a collectible Nuka-Cola item, hinting at potential merchandise tie-ins.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Lucid Outlines Path to Cash Flow Positive and Announces Uber Robotaxi Partnership
items.summary: Luxury electric vehicle maker Lucid has detailed a strategy to achieve cash flow positivity and formed a robotaxi partnership with Uber. Lucid CFO Taoufiq Boussaid discussed the plans on Bloomberg Technology.

items.title: Intel Announces Core Ultra 200S Plus Desktop Processors
items.summary: Intel launched its Core Ultra 200S Plus series desktop processors, marking a significant hardware update. The announcement highlights advancements in non-AI processor technology.

items.title: Marvell Introduces PCIe 8.0 SerDes Technology
items.summary: Marvell showcased its Alaska P PCIe retimers and PCIe 8.0 SerDes technology at DesignCon 2026, advancing high-speed connectivity standards. This development supports faster data transfer in computing hardware.

items.title: Broadcom Delivers First 400G/lane Optical DSP
items.summary: Broadcom announced the industry's first 400G/lane optical DSP, enhancing optical interconnect capabilities for large-scale infrastructure. The technology aims to address limitations in traditional copper connections.

items.title: Tesla Unveils LED-Lit Robotaxi Logo at Austin Pop-Up Event
items.summary: Tesla introduced an active LED-lit Robotaxi logo at its Autonomy Pop-Up event in Austin, TX, on March 13-14, 2026. The feature was first confirmed on-site by attendee @JoeTegtmeyer.

items.title: Google Acquires Intersect Power for $4.75 Billion to Boost Data Center Energy
items.summary: Google completed a $4.75 billion acquisition of independent power producer Intersect to integrate electricity generation with data center operations. The move addresses surging power demands from hyperscalers.
